data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-05T01:36:20.210Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically regarding synchronous LINQ operations.

Let me recall what I know about this topic:

In EF Core with the Cosmos DB provider, synchronous operations have been a pain point. The Cosmos DB provider is inherently async-only because the underlying Azure Cosmos DB SDK is async-only.

In EF Core 8/9, sync operations on the Cosmos provider would throw by default, but there was an opt-in to allow sync-over-async behavior.

In .NET 10 (EF Core 10), there was likely a configuration to opt into sync-over-async for the Cosmos provider.


The trajectory suggests that by .NET 11, the sync-over-async opt-in for the Cosmos provider would be completely removed, forcing all operations to be asynchronous. This represents a breaking change where any code relying on synchronous LINQ queries against Cosmos DB would fail at runtime. The opt-in configuration for sync-over-async is likely being removed entirely in EF Core 11, which would mean all synchronous operations on the Cosmos provider throw without any workaround available. This forces developers to migrate to async APIs completely. The affected methods include `ToList()`, `First()`, `Count()`, and `SaveChanges()`. I should frame this around what users can expect based on the documented deprecation path and advise them to migrate to async methods proactively rather than waiting for a breaking change.
